article.title: Resolve the Conflict Between Efficiency and Resilience

harvestedTerms.summary: MIT Sloan Management Review article on resolving the efficiency-resilience tradeoff in operations, using the airline industry as a primary case study. Introduces three strategies: measuring customer-centric metrics, smart buffer allocation via predictive analytics, and shaping customer options. Limited AI-specific content — primarily an operations management piece with AI/analytics as supporting tools.

article.title: 2026.20: Shifting Alliances in a Changing World

harvestedTerms.summary: A Stratechery weekly roundup by Ben Thompson covering the 'Inference Shift' thesis (agentic vs. answer inference), the Anthropic-xAI compute deal, space data centers, US-China AI relations, and OpenAI's deployment company plans. The key original concept is the distinction between 'answer inference' (human-in-the-loop, latency-sensitive) and 'agentic inference' (autonomous, latency-tolerant), with downstream implications for compute architecture.

A brief quote post from Simon Willison's Weblog featuring Julia Evans on CSS complexity and her approach to learning it seriously. The article has no substantive AI content and yields no trackable AI terms.

article.title: Sony tries to explain that its AI Camera Assistant doesn’t suck

harvestedTerms.summary: Sony defends its AI Camera Assistant feature on the Xperia 1 XIII after criticism that its photo suggestions are poor quality. The article covers a specific consumer product feature that uses AI to suggest exposure, colour, and framing adjustments. Primarily product criticism with limited terminological density.

article.title: Do You Recognize Burnout in Your Organization?

harvestedTerms.summary: An HBR Executive Agenda newsletter item on organizational burnout recognition — no substantive AI content. The article excerpt is almost entirely navigation chrome and subscription boilerplate with no AI terminology, concepts, or relevant framing.
